reasoning: Q3 remains the critical bottleneck at 12.9x slower than DuckDB (1272ms vs 99ms). With one iteration remaining, we must aggressively address this gap. Selecting three complementary Q3 optimizations: zone maps (40-50% speedup, I/O layer), top-K optimization (5-10% speedup, algorithm layer), and eliminate data copies (10-15% speedup, CPU layer). Combined expected improvement: 1272ms → 500-600ms. Zone maps require reingest but unlock massive I/O savings. Risk is acceptable given rollback capability.
focus_areas: Q3 zone maps + mmap optimization (I/O layer)
Q3 eliminate parallel scan data copies (CPU layer)
strategy_notes: Multi-layer optimization strategy targeting Q3's 12.9x performance gap. Zone maps (priority 1) will skip 40-75% of I/O by leveraging sorted date columns. Top-K optimization (priority 2) eliminates wasteful sorting of 299,990 rows. Data copy elimination (priority 3) removes 2-3GB of unnecessary vector allocations in parallel scans. These optimizations are complementary and address different bottlenecks (I/O, algorithm, CPU). Zone maps require reingest - justify this with 40-50% expected speedup. For implementation: (1) Add zone map metadata during ingestion with min/max per 64K-row block, (2) Replace std::sort with std::partial_sort for top-10 selection, (3) Refactor parallel scans to probe hash joins directly without intermediate vector merges.
notes: This is iteration 2/3 - our final opportunity to close Q3's performance gap before the last iteration. Be aggressive but structured. Zone maps are the highest-impact optimization (40-50% speedup) and worth the reingest cost. The three selected optimizations target different layers (I/O, algorithm, CPU) and should compound rather than interfere. Q1 and Q6 are already near-optimal (3.4x and 2.1x gaps), so deprioritize further optimizations there until Q3 is resolved. Expected Q3 outcome: 1272ms → 500-600ms, closing gap from 12.9x to 5-6x relative to DuckDB.
